devid | definition: Unique device identifier used for device-level alignment and tracking across logs.
date | definition: Timestamp of the event, used for temporal alignment and event sequencing.
dname | definition: Device or host name for source device identification and grouping.
logtype | definition: Numeric code representing the type of log, used for filtering and categorizing events.
pri | definition: Priority or severity level of the log event, used for triage and filtering.
ver | definition: Version identifier of the software or log format, aiding in compatibility and schema alignment.
from | definition: Source IP address indicating the origin of the event, used for network path and threat attribution.
mod | definition: Module name within the system that generated the event, used for functional grouping and root cause analysis.
act | definition: Action performed by the module, used for operation-level event correlation.
result | definition: Numeric result or status code of the action, used for success/failure analysis and error tracking.
cmd | definition: Command or operation identifier associated with the event, used for command-level audit and activity grouping.
user | definition: Username associated with the event, used for user activity tracking and privilege auditing.
dsp_msg | definition: Display message providing a human-readable description of the event, may contain additional key=value pairs for detailed alignment.
fwlog | definition: Numeric flag indicating whether the event is a firewall log, used for log type classification and filtering.
Device identifier within the themis system, useful for entity alignment and device-specific grouping.
Timestamp of the logged event, critical for temporal event correlation and session grouping.
Device name or type tag, used for source identification and log source alignment.
Numeric code representing the type of log, enabling log category filtering and classification.
Priority level of the log event, useful for severity-based alerting and event triage.
Software version identifier (nested as ver within a broader string), aiding in version-based correlation and system lineage tracking.
Source IP address of the event origin, essential for network linkage and origin-based grouping.
Module or component name within the themis system, enabling process lineage and module-level tracking.
Action performed (e.g., set, del, 添加), critical for event correlation and operation sequence analysis.
Numeric result code indicating success or failure of the action, used for outcome-based filtering and error analysis.
Command executed, useful for session grouping and command lineage tracking.
Username associated with the event, enabling user-specific session grouping and audit trail analysis.
User-facing display message containing operation details, usable for extracting additional event context and nested field parsing.
Flag or indicator related to firewall logging, used for log type discrimination and correlation with firewall events.
